rationale: The subject property's hip roof (visible in the upper portion of the orange dashed bounding box) displays moderate eave shadow length and a ridge width noticeably narrower than the eave footprint but not dramatically so, both consistent with a 6:12 pitch. The roof facets are visible from the satellite perspective but do not dominate the plan-view footprint as they would at 8:12+. All neighboring homes in this Humble, TX suburban neighborhood share nearly identical hip-roof geometry, facet exposure, and shadow lengths, reinforcing the regional norm of 6:12. The southern Houston-area climate further supports this mid-range pitch as the most common builder standard, with 5:12 being slightly too shallow and 7:12 slightly too steep given the observed shadow and facet proportions.
